title: New release to fix chown errors for otherwise runnable containers.
body:
I am running into the same issue as #7086 and notice that it has been fixed in #7094 with some edits in #7110.  I would really like to be able to run these otherwise runnable containers (preferably without having to do a custom build of podman.)

What needs to happen to get #7094 and #7110 into the next 2.0.X release?

As an additional question what are the timelines for releases? 
Going by release dates it seems to be around one release every 2 weeks?

comments.body: We don't have a definite timeline. We are looking to do a 2.0.4 release including a further batch of bugfixes either late next week or early the following week. That release should include a backport the the patch you reference.

There are two different things going on here.  One is a longer term supported release, which is the 2.0.* release.  This release will be going to RHEL8.3 and Centos 8.3 And any other distros which want to move slower.  Secondly we  will be pushing a newer podman 2.1 release to Fedora, Debian, OpenSUSE, Ubuntu later this month from the master branch.  Once this is done, we hope to stop back porting code to the 2.0.* release.

This coming week, we are planning to put all (most) core developers on a bug fix week, with the hope of closing many issues, bugzillas as well as removing some of the skip* code from the e2e test suite.

This should lead to many more fixes we would like to get out.
Community members are welcome to join the bugfix extravaganza.

> There are two different things going on here. One is a longer term supported release, which is the 2.0.* release. This release will be going to RHEL8.3 and Centos 8.3 And any other distros which want to move slower. Secondly we will be pushing a newer podman 2.1 release to Fedora, Debian, OpenSUSE, Ubuntu later this month from the master branch. Once this is done, we hope to stop back porting code to the 2.0.* release.

Great! As a Fedora user I can look forward to the 2.1 release soon!

> Community members are welcome to join the bugfix extravaganza.

As a Go developer Is there any information regarding the scope of this event?

There is no real scope - next week, the core developers will be going through the Github issues (especially those with the `kind/bug` label), then locating, claiming, and fixing them. Our goal is to greatly reduce the number of open issues and get a large number of bugfixes in before the next release.

@mheon Can you confirm that #7094 was included in this release?  I looked through the diff for 2.0.3 and 2.0.4 and didn't see this included.

Please let me know if I overlooked it.  In anycase I would like to reopen this as it seems that 2.0.4 doesn't actually fix the issue.

https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/5d790bb2be77fae2de429d1004bec5fed0c55ba8
is included in the branch, so the fix is definitely in 2.0.4
